title: Turraea pulchella
taxonomy: Turraea pulchella is a species of plant in the family Simaroubaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the regions of tropical West and Central Africa. The classification of Turraea pulchella includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, class as Magnoliopsida, and order as Sapindales. This plant is further classified into the family Simaroubaceae and the genus Turraea. Turraea pulchella is a species within the Turraea genus.
uses: Turraea pulchella has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. Its bark and leaves are used to treat different ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as a remedy for snake bites.
